L1.area: Authentication protocol response and redirect processing

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where redirect URIs in authentication protocol requests are not validated against allowed URI schemes. An attacker can craft a request containing a redirect URI with a dangerous scheme (such as a JavaScript URI), which is then used in a form-based POST response without proper sanitization, allowing execution of arbitrary scripts in the victim's browser session.

L3.area: OIDC protocol redirect URI validation and resolution utilities
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists where redirect URIs in SAML and OIDC authentication protocol requests are not validated against allowed URI schemes. When a wildcard redirect pattern is configured, an attacker can supply a redirect URI with a non-HTTP scheme (such as a JavaScript URI), which bypasses scheme validation and is rendered in a form-based POST response, allowing execution of arbitrary scripts in the victim's browser session.

ground_truth.reason: The redirect URI validation does not check that the URI scheme is restricted to HTTP(S) or explicitly allowed by configured patterns, allowing an attacker to inject a JavaScript URI via a crafted SAML or OIDC request, resulting in XSS when the URI is used in a form POST response.
